title: Update vendor containers/common
body:
This fixes a FreeBSD regression which prevented creating new containers.

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@ashley-cui I would like to get something like this into podman's v4.6 branch but I'm not sure what the right way is. Should I merge the specific commit in c/common which fixes my problem into common's v0.55 branch first?
@dfr Yep! best way is to open a PR against c/common's v0.55 branch, backporting the fix to the branch.

> @dfr Yep! best way is to open a PR against c/common's v0.55 branch, backporting the fix to the branch.

Done, thanks!

What is the best way forward for this one - @TomSweeneyRedHat suggested using common v0.56 but the branch doesn't exist in c/common yet. I would like to get this (or something like it) merged into main soon - at the moment podman builds from main on freebsd can't create new containers.
Looks like c/common was already updated so I'm closing this PR.
